background: Optimize a bounded two-variable scientific response using prior scientific experience.
objective: Maximize the target response while avoiding unstable operating regions.
core_experience: The response is usually stronger when x1 is moderately high.
Very high x2 can reduce robustness even when the immediate response looks good.
Good candidates should balance expected improvement with conservative movement from known good settings.
expert_rules: Treat the experience as soft prior knowledge.
Do not ignore measured history when it contradicts the prior.
failure_modes: Candidates near the edge of the box may be unstable.
A single high observation should not define the whole search region.
constraints: Keep all variables inside the declared bounds.
measurement_notes: The objective is noisy at the second decimal place.
prompt_guidance: Prefer concise numeric outputs that the optimizer can parse.
Use domain experience to rank plausible regions, but leave final selection to BO.
labo_prompt_addendum: For LABO, predict one numeric low-fidelity target for every candidate point.
lgbo_prompt_addendum: For LGBO, return either one point or one compact region with confidence.
